subject: Concerns re HB 542 and public safety
body: Thank you for the opportunity to comment on HB 542. We are concerned that changing mandatory minimum sentences for certain drug offenses could undermine public safety. We urge caution and request stronger safeguards. While we appreciate the intent to allow more judge discretion, we believe mandatory minimum standards should remain for fentanyl trafficking. We would welcome a meeting next week to discuss data.

subject: Support for HB 542: restoring judge discretion
body: Our office supports HB 542 because restoring judge discretion will reduce recidivism and allow proportional sentences in non-violent drug offenses. We can share case examples. Happy to meet when convenient.

subject: HB 542 feedback and suggested safeguards (ASAP)
body: We recognize the value of alternatives to mandatory minimum sentences, but ask for safeguards to protect public safety, especially in fentanyl cases. We need to review proposed amendments ASAP and suggest victim notification requirements.

subject: Town hall on HB 542?
body: I support the bill and appreciate your work to give judges more discretion. When is the next town hall?

subject: Brief requested: HB 542
body: Please send a brief on HB 542 summarizing impacts on mandatory minimum sentences and any data on recidivism. Neutral at this stage; we are compiling notes for the Governor.
